synced:
[00:07.63] When I was six years old, I broke my leg
[00:14.64] When I was running from my brother and his friends
[00:21.74] And tasted the sweet perfume of the mountain grass I rolled down
[00:29.11] Well, I was younger then, take me back to when
[00:34.56] I found my heart and broke it here
[00:39.29] Made friends and lost them through the years
[00:43.46] And I've not seen the roaring fields in so long, I know I've grown
[00:50.59] But I can't wait to go home
[00:53.37] I'm on my way
[00:56.86] Driving at 90 down those country lanes
[01:04.19] Singing to "Tiny Dancer"
[01:07.25] And I miss the way you make me feel, and it's real
[01:14.52] And we watched the sunset over the castle on the hill
[01:22.75] 
[01:25.72] 15 years old and smoking hand-rolled cigarettes
[01:32.88] Running from the law through the backfields
[01:35.67] And getting drunk with my friends
[01:39.94] Had my first kiss on a Friday night
[01:43.76] I don't reckon that I did it right
[01:46.75] But I was younger then, take me back to when
[01:52.74] We found weekend jobs, when we got paid
[01:57.80] We'd buy cheap spirits and drink them straight
[02:01.36] Me and my friends have not thrown up in so long, oh, how we've grown
[02:08.61] But I can't wait to go home
[02:11.57] I'm on my way (hoo-hoo)
[02:15.13] Driving at 90 down those country lanes (hoo-hoo)
[02:22.18] Singing to "Tiny Dancer"
[02:25.51] And I miss the way you make me feel, and it's real
[02:32.44] And we watched the sunset (hoo-hoo), over the castle on the hill
[02:40.42] Hoo-hoo, over the castle on the hill
[02:47.61] Hoo-hoo, over the castle on the hill
[02:55.02] 
[03:01.86] One friend left to sell clothes, one works down by the coast
[03:09.06] One had two kids but lives alone, one's brother overdosed
[03:16.05] One's already on his second wife, one's just barely getting by
[03:22.41] But these people raised me and I can't wait to go home
[03:29.57] And I'm on my way
[03:33.32] I still remember these old country lanes
[03:39.89] When we did not know the answers
[03:43.93] And I miss the way (hoo-hoo) you make me feel, it's real
[03:50.72] When we watched the sunset (hoo-hoo) over the castle on the hill
[03:58.67] Hoo-hoo, over the castle on the hill
[04:05.99] Hoo-hoo, over the castle on the hill
[04:11.90]
plain:
When I was six years old, I broke my leg
When I was running from my brother and his friends
And tasted the sweet perfume of the mountain grass I rolled down
Well, I was younger then, take me back to when

I found my heart and broke it here
Made friends and lost them through the years
And I've not seen the roaring fields in so long, I know I've grown
But I can't wait to go home

I'm on my way
Driving at 90 down those country lanes
Singing to "Tiny Dancer"
And I miss the way you make me feel, and it's real
And we watched the sunset over the castle on the hill

15 years old and smoking hand-rolled cigarettes
Running from the law through the backfields
And getting drunk with my friends
Had my first kiss on a Friday night
I don't reckon that I did it right
But I was younger then, take me back to when

We found weekend jobs, when we got paid
We'd buy cheap spirits and drink them straight
Me and my friends have not thrown up in so long, oh, how we've grown
But I can't wait to go home

I'm on my way (hoo-hoo)
Driving at 90 down those country lanes (hoo-hoo)
Singing to "Tiny Dancer"
And I miss the way you make me feel, and it's real
And we watched the sunset (hoo-hoo), over the castle on the hill

Hoo-hoo, over the castle on the hill
Hoo-hoo, over the castle on the hill

One friend left to sell clothes, one works down by the coast
One had two kids but lives alone, one's brother overdosed
One's already on his second wife, one's just barely getting by
But these people raised me and I can't wait to go home

And I'm on my way
I still remember these old country lanes
When we did not know the answers
And I miss the way (hoo-hoo) you make me feel, it's real
When we watched the sunset (hoo-hoo) over the castle on the hill

Hoo-hoo, over the castle on the hill
Hoo-hoo, over the castle on the hill
